tweets.text:
The AI boom is transforming one of the biggest deflationary forces into a key source of US inflation:

Import prices for computer and electronic products surged +7.4% in the first 6 months of 2026, to 77.1 points, the highest since January 2016.

This also marks a +8.0% YoY jump, the largest annual increase on record.

This represents a historic reversal from a downtrend which persisted for over two decades.

Between 2006 and 2019, computer and electronic product import prices fell -30%, as improving technology and manufacturing efficiency consistently pushed costs lower.

Now, the AI investment boom is straining manufacturing capacity for semiconductors, servers, and electronic components, as companies race to build out AI infrastructure regardless of cost.

The AI revolution has flipped technology from a long-term source of deflation into a major driver of inflation.

tweets.text:
.@AppliedInt Co-founder @qasar: "Our mission is to put intelligence on a billion machines, and we think that can have a profound impact on society."

"We've seen the unlock in the digital world, and everyone's super excited about it, and you have trillion-dollar companies emerging."

"When we look back in 25 years, the companies that impact the physical world might actually be bigger than the companies that impact the digital world."

tweets.text:
The global helium supply chain is tightening:

China's helium imports from Qatar have collapsed to near zero, from a peak of ~65 million cubic feet in late-2025, the lowest on record.

Imports from Russia have declined -50 million cubic feet over the last 2 quarters, to ~38.5 million cubic feet, the lowest since Q3 2025.

The drop comes as the Iran War disrupts exports from Qatar, which supplied ~33% of global helium before the conflict,

As a result, spot helium prices for immediate delivery have roughly DOUBLED over the last 4 months.

Meanwhile, China has introduced new export controls on helium, despite not being a major producer itself, as the country relies heavily on imports to supply critical industries, including semiconductor manufacturing and medical equipment.

China has also been an important transit route for Russian helium into Europe since direct EU imports from Russia were restricted.

Helium supply is being squeezed from multiple directions.

The drop comes as the Iran War disrupts exports from Qatar, which supplied ~33% of global helium before the conflict,

As a result, spot helium prices for immediate delivery have roughly DOUBLED over the last 4 months.

China has also been an important transit route for Russian helium into Europe since direct EU imports from Russia were restricted.

tweets.text:
Today, Applied Intuition is launching Dana, an agentic platform for developing physical AI applications.

Co-founders Qasar Younis and Peter Ludwig join a16z's Marc Andreessen and Erik Torenberg to discuss their mission to put intelligence on one billion machines:

01:34 Physical AI vs digital AI
05:28 Humans as a design constraint
10:30 Beyond robotaxis and humanoids
19:04 What killed Cruise
28:19 The chip maker playbook
35:26 When self-driving is the default
45:14 Nobody wants to drive trucks
49:54 Introducing Dana
1:00:54 "World model" means 100 things
1:05:28 GTA 6 may be the last of its kind
1:15:40 Fear of AI is a luxury

@qasar @AppliedInt @pmarca @eriktorenberg

tweets.text:
BREAKING: President Trump is preparing to announce 10% tariffs on "dozens of countries" this week, per FT.

Details include:

1. These new tariffs come as President Trump's current 10% tariffs expire later this week

2. President Trump's advisors have reportedly warned him against doing so

3. Advisors are reportedly worried about risking the economic shocks of the original trade war ahead of the midterm elections

4. The Trump Admin is also working on other investigations that could grant it the legal authority to propose higher tariffs

More tariffs are on the way.

tweets.text:
THESIS/PREDICTION: I've thought about this for a while and have come to the conclusion that Warren Buffett et al could/should choose Trump Accounts as the destination for donating their stock shares. This would kill multiple birds with one stone:
1. Immediately shrink wealth gap (esp if they target low income zip codes like Dell did) bc in the end almost ALL of their wealth is via stocks (cos they founded or invest in, see chart). While $1000 into SPYM is great, better than nothing, it just won't can't catch the compounding of mega rich.
2. Increase financial literacy - Everyone is for this and by donating stock it will induce ppl to learn about markets, investments etc, encourage capitalism over other economic theories that they'll be inundated w/ in college.
3. Curb the rise of 'redistribution' economic policies/politicians- might as well do it now like a hero bf you have to in the form of taxes or seizure. And to be fair the gov't effectively subsidizes the stock mkt through intervention and money printing which is totally regressive so this is way to level that out a bit.
4. Expand legacy - esp for Buffett who is 95, by gifting Berkshire shares to children they will grow up Berkshire investors. He'd be hero to them. 
5. No mismanagement/skimming/cap gains tax - one issue with charitable donations is the end cause/ppl only sees a portion- esp if the govt is involved. Here, 100% of your donation goes directly to the intended party. And no cap gains tax to boot.

tweets.text:
Starting a business has never been cheaper, and AI could make it cheaper still.

In 2004, Tobi Lutke tried to sell snowboards online and found the tools too clunky and expensive. He built his own instead. Shopify launched in 2006, letting founders reach a global audience for roughly $1,000 a year.

Since then, Shopify has bundled payments, logistics, and capital into one platform. About 70% of Shopify's Gross Merchandise Value now runs through Shopify Payments, pooling volume to bring merchants rates once reserved for large retailers.

Shopify Capital loans range from $200 to $2 million, a spread traditional banks likely cannot serve profitably. Originations more than doubled over the two years ended 2025, even as Small Business Administration (SBA) lending fell roughly 15%.

ARK's research suggests AI is now compressing a third cost: labor. Shopify's Sidekick agent functions like an always-on CFO or COO. On average serial entrepreneurs already generate more than 2x the sales of first-time founders after their first sale.

tweets.text: On this day in 2010, President Obama signed the Dodd-Frank Wall Street Reform and Consumer Protection Act, the most sweeping overhaul of U.S. financial regulation since the Great Depression. The law was a direct response to the 2008 crisis, which had wiped out Lehman Brothers, forced a $700 billion taxpayer bailout of the banking system, and erased trillions in household wealth. Dodd-Frank created the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau, established a council to monitor system-wide risk, imposed the Volcker Rule restricting banks from trading with their own capital, and pushed derivatives onto regulated exchanges. The act ran 848 pages and required more than 390 separate rulemakings across a dozen federal agencies, a build-out that took the better part of a decade to finish. Consumer refunds and relief credited to the bureau it created have since topped $20 billion.
tweets.full_text: On this day in 2010, President Obama signed the Dodd-Frank Wall Street Reform and Consumer Protection Act, the most sweeping overhaul of U.S. financial regulation since the Great Depression. The law was a direct response to the 2008 crisis, which had wiped out Lehman Brothers, forced a $700 billion taxpayer bailout of the banking system, and erased trillions in household wealth. Dodd-Frank created the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau, established a council to monitor system-wide risk, imposed the Volcker Rule restricting banks from trading with their own capital, and pushed derivatives onto regulated exchanges. The act ran 848 pages and required more than 390 separate rulemakings across a dozen federal agencies, a build-out that took the better part of a decade to finish. Consumer refunds and relief credited to the bureau it created have since topped $20 billion.

tweets.text:
Interesting interview of Nigel Eccles, who cofounded Fanduel: 
"I think within five years AI dealers will represent over 80% of the live dealer market. The speed of product improvement here is going to stun people.
-“We already knew that our AI dealer product was a hit with players. However, when we started talking to operators, what we discovered was the enthusiasm amongst operators is even greater than players.”
-“We honestly aren’t really seeing any major hurdles,” he said. “Player and operator demand is there. Technology is moving blazingly fast.”
tweets.full_text:
Interesting interview of Nigel Eccles, who cofounded Fanduel: 
"I think within five years AI dealers will represent over 80% of the live dealer market. The speed of product improvement here is going to stun people.
-“We already knew that our AI dealer product was a hit with players. However, when we started talking to operators, what we discovered was the enthusiasm amongst operators is even greater than players.”
-“We honestly aren’t really seeing any major hurdles,” he said. “Player and operator demand is there. Technology is moving blazingly fast.”

thread_tweets.text:
🍁With trade tensions heating back up — Trump has vowed a fresh 50% tariff on some Canadian goods, invoking Section 338 of the 1930 Tariff Act (a Depression-era provision never before used for tariffs) to hit ~$20B of imports like milk, beer, hockey equipment and plywood, while sparing energy, potash and critical minerals — here's what the data is actually saying:

☑️  Canada's effective tariff rate sits near 3.1% — below Mexico's ~3.7%. Yet Canada's imports stalled through the 2025 escalation while Mexico's climbed to a record ~$55B in May.

☑️  Since 2016, Mexico is up ~140% vs Canada's ~63%. They started roughly equal — Mexico has pulled decisively ahead.

☑️  Why? Canada's headline number is dominated by energy, which moves on price, not policy. Strip it out and the tariffs are clearly biting where they're aimed: steel & aluminum down ~40% since 2022 ($3.9B→$2.4B), autos sliding (as low as $2.6B in Jan), lumber halved.

☑️  Meanwhile Canada has no nearshoring engine — machinery/electronics is flat (~$3.5B), the exact category booming for Mexico.

Bottom line: Mexico is winning nearshoring; Canada is absorbing tariffs on metals and autos — with nothing structural to offset it. Same trade war, opposite trajectories.

📊 ECAN US TARIFFS<GO> + WSL TARIFFS<GO> — U.S. trade flows and effective tariff rates by partner, updated monthly on @TheTerminal

tweets.text:
Transfer activity of tokenized stocks is on the rise. Monthly transfer volume for tokenized stocks reached $9.22 billion in June, up from $53 million last June — a more than 170x increase. This metric measures any onchain movements, such as trading, transfers between wallets, or collateral deposits into DeFi protocols.

The market for tokenized stocks is still a small one compared to traditional equities, which trade in the double-digit trillions per month. But the trend is clear: More issuers and platforms are bringing tokenized stocks online, and the category is growing.
tweets.full_text:
Transfer activity of tokenized stocks is on the rise. Monthly transfer volume for tokenized stocks reached $9.22 billion in June, up from $53 million last June — a more than 170x increase. This metric measures any onchain movements, such as trading, transfers between wallets, or collateral deposits into DeFi protocols.

The market for tokenized stocks is still a small one compared to traditional equities, which trade in the double-digit trillions per month. But the trend is clear: More issuers and platforms are bringing tokenized stocks online, and the category is growing.

tweets.text:
IBM's -25% decline in a single trading day last week was the biggest ever for the 115-year-old company and a 15-sigma event.

That basically means it should not have happened even once in the history of the universe.

But it did, and given enough time it will happen again, because financial markets don't follow a normal distribution.

$IBM

tweets.text:
Platinum is entering its weakest seasonal stretch already in a downtrend.

It has fallen 40% from its January high.

Since 1987, holding platinum only during this window has resulted in a cumulative price decline of 57%.

In 2026, the window runs from the July 17 close through October 7.

Read full analysis: https://t.co/SPC7H0mvUt

tweets.text:
Stocks ran while investors got less excited.

In 75 sessions, sentiment went from +0.30 to -0.33 while the S&P 500 gained nearly 19%.
 After similar setups, the index was higher 71% of the time over 12 months with a median gain of about 9%.

Through 6 months, the best gain and worst loss were nearly the same.

Read full analysis: https://t.co/PqcFgylsw3
